title: Chemical Engineer (Material Planning I Operations I Safety)
description:
• Well Established Company
  • Basic $3200 - $3800 + AWS + Variable Bonus
  • Excellent Welfare and Benefits + Career progression
  • Working location: Tuas (Transport Provided)
  • Working days: Monday to Friday
  • Working hours: 8am to 5pm


Job Responsibilities:

  • To supervise and manage the functions of Repacking/Containment/ICW operations and Maintenance
  • To monitor the safe process flow and backlog level of TIW waste
  • Ensure all staff are well trained according to OJT programme and following the safe work procedures and SOPs
  • To ensure the effectiveness and smoothness of coordination with operation and sales team and customer service maintaining high quality of services
  • To monitor the outgoing containment purchase budget and encourage the recycling of used containment to save cost
  • Daily and regular inspection of the operation processes and monitor the plant 5S and TPM activities, safety and discipline at works, machineries and equipment conditions
  • Lead the daily safety toolbox meeting and regular inspection of PPE items that staffs at workplaces are enough for their safety
  • Conduct periodic review of existing procedures and workflow of plant operations and functions in lines with cost effectiveness with maximum throughput
  • Apply continues improvement or modification plans to maintain an excellent operational model.
  • Work with Lab chemists to evaluate and develop new treatment methods and handing procedures for new and unknown wastes, and review of operation costs and regular update to management
  • To ensure EHS compliance through the continual maintenance and improvement of ISO 45001 implementation, and always maintain good housekeeping of site
  • To prepare and analyze daily and monthly operation records/reports (e.g., cost, productivity, quality, non-conformance, etc) on plant processing and maintenance. And follow up necessary corrective actions and monitor consistently to relevant department staffs to follow up closely
  • To compile annual budget of plant operations and Maintenance for Director’s approval and to ensure expenditures are well within the budget allotment
  • To work closely with operation staffs and engineering staffs to develop and implement preventive maintenance schedule for operations and maintenance of machinery and equipment
  • Regular briefing / training to be conducted to department staffs for safety handling of repacking loose waste, MSDS, PPE requirement and risk assessment before operations
  • To activate emergency response team of operation and maintenance staffs and response to any in house spill and fire incident if in case of emergency
  • To liaise with EHS department to ensure fellow dept staffs are upkeep with method of handling and emergency equipment usages in terms of spills and fire incident and to conduct regular practice and drill exercises with different scenarios to workers so that they are well familiar in emergency to response in time


Requirements:

  • Degree in Chemical Engineering or Chemistry
  • At least 2 year relevant experience


Candidates are encouraged to apply this position via Apply Now button with the following information in the resume

  • Work experiences and job responsibilities
  • Current and Expected salary
  • Reason for leaving
  • Date of availability
  • Education background


We regret that only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.


THIEN CHING MEI (R2198032)

EA Recruitment Pte Ltd

EA License No: 21C0492
